entry.title: Re: how can I have a dynamic text with dimension input co...
entry.content:
Hi Simon,

It seems the customer expects dynamic text to be available on input controls within the popup window. Currently, dynamic text can be applied to filters in the popup, but not to input controls. To achieve dynamic text on input controls in the popup, separate input controls need to be created within the popup itself, as those created at the story level cannot be used.

Thanks,

Mastan

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports - Issue with bold and can grow op...
entry.content: This would be tidy, but is not necessary in this case. If the condition does not match, there is a fallback to "regular" (=0)

entry.title: Re: Issue with concatenate. No blanks at the end. RESPECTING BLANKS not working fully.
entry.content:
Hi @prakashjasti ,
I tried you suggestion. Its not working. For GUI_DOWNLOAD, the suggestion that Sandra gave me is good, and it is resolving the issue, however I still have the same issue when I use TRANSFER.
Thanks for your input anyway.
BR.

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Cheat Sheet: Amounts and Quantities in ABAP CDS
entry.content:
Hi @bharatbajaj,

 In your blog post, I saw the use of CalculatedIUnit to achieve this calculation, which is bit strange in my opinion, as we are still trying to get a Calculated AMOUNT (and not quantity), (Amount / Quantity -> gives Amount), so why we mandatorily need a separate unit reference and not just use the CUKY reference instead? Think of the rental price of an apartment. 600 Euro for a 60m2 flat makes a price of 10 Euro per m2. So the unit reference is Euro/square meter. CUKY references must have one of the values from the database table TCURC, so they can be only, for example, Euro. That is not suitable for example of the apartment price PER M2.

Moreover, if we have multiple calculated amounts, then for each Amount field calculation, I need a separate calculatedUnit (having the same or different value). 

Consider the following example: 

Currency,
@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: ‘Currency’
Case when Land = DE then sales else null as SalesDE,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: ‘Currency’
Case when Land = US then sales else null as SalesUS,

=>  different values would have to be written to the currency field for one line. This would lead to a short dump in certain scenarios. Generally spoken, the logic is that a key figure is a tuple consisting of its number and a unit (currency, unit or complex unit). Therefore, each key figure (especially formula) should refer to its own unit field.

I wonder, why so much complexity in CDS for a simple calculation.

The additional checks provide more type safety and identify problematic situations early on. if you divide a CURR by a QUAN (eg. LV_AMOUNT = BSEG-DMBTR / BSEG-MENGE), then you need some reference to provide a semantic sense to your result. The stricter CDS checks support you with that.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: SAP Crystal Reports - Issue with bold and can grow op...
entry.content: Thanks for your input. I tried it too, but I have the same behaviour. Field increase his width automatically and text appears clipped.

entry.title: Re: How we can maintain location HANA Public cloud.
entry.content: Hello Lucy, Thank you quick reply.

entry.title: S/4HANA for Public Cloud: error message EU519 "You are not authorized... S_DEVELOP)"
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently working with SAP Public Cloud Edition Professional Services and have encountered an issue that I am unable to resolve. I would appreciate any help or guidance regarding the error I am facing.

 

Due to an authorization issue, the customizing for revenue account determination customizing cannot be finished. The error occurs during the customizing of the app "SIMG" ("Implementation Activities," i.e., the customizing). The following screenshots were taken in the Customizing tenant.

Reproducing the error:

1 - First, a new condition table 501 was created in customizing.

 

 

 

2 - It is a standard customizing setting, where a created condition table must be modified again. This is done via the customizing jump "Change Condition Tables for Revenue Account Determination."

 

3 - Enter the table name and "Change."

 

 

4 - Click on "Generate."

 

5 - This message appears, but it does not seem like the source of the coming problem.

 

 

6 - Click on "Generated Object."

 

7 - Switch to change mode:

 

8 - This message appears, preventing changes: “You are not authorized to make changes (authorization object S_DEVELOP)” – Message no EU519

 

The authorization object S_DEVELOP is an authorization object o SAP GUI, i.e., an object that would be defined in the PFCG for the role. It cannot be changed by the Identity Management apps of SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud edition where restriction types are use.

 

This means that a separate authorization system must exist for the settings within the app "SIMG"? But where?

 

We tried the same in the Development system. As soon the customizing for changing the condition table ist startet the following message appears.

 

Thank you in advance for your support!

Best regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Special GL
entry.content: Start with OBXR

entry.title: Embedding S4 Custom Fiori app inside BTP Fiori app
entry.content:
Hello Expert ,

i would like to embed/Load based S4 Hana Custom Fiori App in side BTP hosted freestyle Fiori App in a container /Iframe , was investigating if this is doable , flow is that user wants to load  the S4 deployed Fiori app from the Custom Ui5 app deployed in BTP based on some criteria with some parameter on click of a button on next screen which will be detail screen wrapping up the embedded Fiori app from S4 by passing the required URL parameter , is there any way this can be achieved trough standard services on BTP or is it doable ?

Any point of view or suggestions are most welcome.

Thanks,
Siddharth

Hello @gowthamigunji2000

https://help.sap.com/docs/forms-service-by-adobe/sap-forms-service-neo/using-adobe-livecycle-designer 

Additionally follow below KBA and other blogs 

https://me.sap.com/notes/2791338 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-s-4-hana-cloud-testing-form-developments-in-adobe-livecycle-designer/ba-p/13416953 

Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Knowledge Exchange B1 Production Module
entry.content:
Hello, first of all, we need to understand the size of your company, its location, its industrial sector and then the complexity of your production system.

Without knowing this data or having more knowledge, I doubt that anyone can even answer you.

With the small production module plus the MRP, you can complete a production system that does not require excessive complexity.

entry.title: Re: SAP AIF Index table always stores first occurrence of E1EDK14-ORGID in key fields. Can't store V
entry.content:
The determination of key fields by qualifier was released with the following notes:

3416275 - Qualifier for Key Fields

3456899 - Key Field Qualifier: Key field qualifier for single index table fields

Both notes are required to enable the feature.

entry.title: Re: Not Able to create logos or Texts in output managemen...
entry.content:
Hello @gowthamigunji2000 ,

I hope you must have proper authorization for catalog SAP_CORE_BC_OM. Check the app documentation -> Implementation Information -> Configuration.

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F2761')/S33 

Also refer to the below as well, which clearly says editing custom texts and logos are only permitted in development system.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003102179 

Best Regards,

Vijayendra

entry.title: Re: Key User Extensibility in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Sales
entry.content:
Hi @josua,
If a standard field is not available in an API, you cannot replace it with a custom field because all follow up processes will happen based on the standard field. If there are no follow up processes for that standard field or if they are not needed for your process because you just want to print it or for some statistical purposes, you can also use a custom field for it.

But in most cases, you need to request the field to be added to the API via customer confluence portal.

best regards,
Christian

entry.title: System functionality for the employees on secondment
entry.content:
Hello,

I have received a request from a client asking if there is a native functionality allowing for the home country HR to control and manage the home and host record of an employee who is sent for the secondment. Host country HR should not be able to access the record of a person on the secondment.

I know this is possible to be set with RBPs, but the client insists on having the SAP response if there is native functionality supporting this behavior.

Does SuccessFactors has a native functionality which meets the above described requirement?

Regards,

Piotr Retecki

entry.title: Re: Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere
How to give access only to particular systems for Cloud ALM users
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently a CloudALM user who is having read only access can view all other systems integrated with CloudALM.
We would like to know whether it is possible to give access only to particular systems for Cloud ALM users ?

Please let me know if you require further details.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to give access only to particular systems for Clo...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

By enabling access control, you can restrict the access of specific landscape objects (services, systems, and business services) to specific users.

For more information, please see Access Control.
You can find more information under Access Control in the SAP Cloud ALM Expert Portal. 

Best regards,
Ellie
SAP Support

entry.title: Employment end date notification - old alert
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have some concerns regarding the notifications for employment end dates in cases of contract renewal and transformation from limited to unlimited.

Currently, we set the effective date as the day after the first contract expiration date. For example, if the employment end date is 31/12/2024, the effective date for both renewal and transformation time slice is 01/01/2025.

However, we face a problem when trying to delete the old pending alerts. If we perform the action today, 12/09/2024, the delete old pending alert fails because the effective date of the change is 01/01/2025 and there are pending alerts not deleted.

Furthermore, when transforming a limited contract to an unlimited one, SAP suggests using 9999-12-31 as the end date. However, same here the effective date needs to be earlier than the trigger date of the old pending alert.

To illustrate, let's consider the previous example. By adding a time slice with the effective date set to 12/09/2024 and using either 2999 (for limited to unlimited) or the new date, the old pending alerts are deleted. However, by doing so and adding time slice as a data change only to adjust the data and prevent old pending alert to be send out, the employment end date is no longer reportable. (we can still can the time slice either review or limited – unlimited on the effective date 01/01/2025)

If anyone has encountered this issue or knows of any best practices to handle it, your insights would be greatly appreciated.

Thank

Giorgio

Below the KBA already consulted 

3071514 - Delete Old Pending Alert Flag - Same Effective Date - SAP for Me

How Old Pending Alerts Are Handled | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: How to avoid creating multiple detailed to one merged dimension
entry.content:
Is there a way to avoid creating multiple detailed objects to one merged dimension? 

Query 1: (Order level) Order Number, customer name

Query 2: (Shipment level) many objects 

I need the report to be order level but most of the objects I need are from shipment level. I have a merged dimension on the order level. I am creating many different variables with detail to the merged dimension and am looking to see if there is a way to avoid this duplicate operation.

kind regards

Thangaraj Thomas

entry.title: Re: SAP Advanced Shipping and Receiving : Discover the challenges faced during the implementation of
entry.content:
Hi,

regarding 1:

You can use ASR and LDAP in one EWM.

You need one document type for ASR-Warehouse Requests and one document type for LDAP warehouse requests.

entry.title: Re: How to exclude PO from MRP calculation in S4 Hana Pub...
entry.content:
Hello DominikTylczyn,
I really appreciate your help on this. Let me explain the situation. Our inventory capacity is very limited, and we consume goods on a weekly basis through process orders. The lead time to deliver goods is 120 days, almost 5 months excluding weekends. If I were to implement MRP today, it would deliver goods after 5 months, so the gap between today and 5 months from now would need to be manually filled. Therefore, the purchase orders created during this time frame should not be considered, otherwise the system will not generate purchase requests due to the shortage in inventory and the low Reorder Point (ROP). I hope this clarifies the situation.

entry.title: B2B for SAP Customer Data Platform
entry.content:
While the value proposition of a customer data platform from the B2C space, i.e. providing a unified view of customer data from multiple sources, enabling real-time insights, enhancing customer experiences, and better decision-making across the organization hold true for B2B as well, it takes more to unlock these values for B2B than for B2C. Which will then enable the delivery of an enhanced account-based customer engagement experience that is shared and consistent across front-office and back-office.

SAP Customer Data Platform helps you to unlock these values for your B2B business while offering some unique advantages.  

  1. Native ERP integration: SAP Customer Data Platform has a native connection with its S/4HANA ERP system, which is especially valuable for B2B scenarios. This integration, e.g., allows for prioritized order fulfillment, supply chain optimization, and product innovation based on customer data. This tight integration with S/4HANA back-office solution is a unique strength of SAP's offering. The back-office also happens to be a great source to identify potential risk within your customer base. Know and manage customer issues or mitigate potential risk in a proactive manner. 


  2. B2B and B2C in one solution. SAP Customer Data Platform offers the flexibility to support B2B and B2C business models within the same platform. This can be beneficial for companies operating in both or mixed business models. SAP Customer Data Platform also offers an authorization-based separation of data and business processes in this case. 

  3. Industry-specific AI models: SAP Customer Data Platform offers AI models that are specifically optimized for industry use cases. This allows for customer behavior prediction like true customer lifetime value, churn risk and propensity to buy.

  4. Privacy and consent management: SAP Customer Data Platform puts a strong emphasis on maintaining the purpose of customer data during every engagement and respecting customer data privacy. This is crucial in B2B relationships where data governance and compliance are often stringent.

What specific B2B features does SAP Customer Data Platform offer?

  1. Profiling of B2B contacts and accounts while maintaining multiple relationships including details like job role with a flexible, customer extensible data model. Aggregation of contacts across the account provides one holistic view of the customer across the enterprise. SAP Customer Data Platform manages contacts as profiles, accounts as groups and their relationships in a parent-child manner.


  2. Accounts can be managed in multiple levels of a hierarchy, e.g. team, department, and organization.

  3. Identity resolution for accounts as well as contacts to create a unified view. The matching rules will determine how to match the data to an existing account, or create a new one, based on a prioritized match and merge configuration. Define your own single or multi-attribute match rules. For example based on exact match for Account ID, eMail Domain or DUNS Number.

  4. 3rd Party Data enrichment for firmographics and intent data. You can enrich your account and contact data with further data from external data providers like Dun & Bradstreet, Bombora, Data Axle, ZoomInfo and others. Examples include company size and industry a company operates in or interest in certain products. Maintain data integrity and identify the best leads meeting the criteria of your ideal customer profile. Get the highest quality and most likely to close prospects in the hands of sales as early as possible. SAP Customer Data Platform offers pre-build integration to above mentioned B2B data providers as well as a flexible Connector Studio to integrate with other providers.


  5. B2B insights generation. SAP Customer Data Platform allows you to calculate activity indicators and segments on contact as well as on all account levels. This is especially helpful for the more complex B2B lead-to-cash process. Use profiling and scoring features to manage an automated qualification and decisioning process, identify and mitigate risk or find your best advocates. 

  6. CX Journeys for accounts. SAP Customer Data Platform allows you to model the progression of your accounts and their opportunities through the lead-to-cash process and helps you automate it. Qualify and nurture prospects, welcome new customers using an onboarding journey, mitigate risk throughout the front-office or enable any number of account-based customer journeys. Journey milestones can activate marketing, sales and service simultaneously to provide a consistent and improved customer experience. 

  7. B2B specific out-of-the-box integration with
    • SAP S/4HANA
    • SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2
    • SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement

SAP Customer Data Platform supports B2B throughout its entire platform from data ingestion to activation of the next best action. Protect data privacy and purpose, create unified customer views, enrich contacts and accounts, generate and share insights throughout the organization, activate customer data based processes in real-time or have unified customer data available for querying.

 

Key B2B Use Cases SAP Customer Data Platform Enables

Real-Time Revenue Identification

Leverage SAP Customer Data Platform to identify potential revenue opportunities in real-time across the customer base. Use the indicator, segments and orchestration features to develop an always-on model focused on getting the highest quality prospects to sales early, identifying new opportunity for existing customers and finding the best targets for higher value account-based engagements.   

Proactively Identify and Manage Risk

While the goal is to always have customers 100% satisfied, operations or factors beyond your control do happen. Risk does not occur overnight; it can fester and evolve over time. Often, the front-office is completely unaware that something has gone wrong. ERP data can be the source of both positive and negative events throughout the customer lifecycle. Just as revenue opportunities can always be found, potential risk can be also. Risk mitigation can and should be managed by marketing, sales, and service.

Account Based Marketing and Sales Enablement

SAP Customer Data Platform helps you to target and engage specific high-value accounts in a personalized manner rather than casting a wide net. With its orchestration capabilities and flexible connectivity, even prebuilt to SAP Sales and Service Cloud Version 2 and SAP Emarsys Customer Engagement, it enables a close collaboration between sales and marketing teams and helps keep the message consistent across channels to different contacts of the same account. 

Account-Based Personalization

SAP Customer Data Platform unlocks new personalized engagement scenarios. Drive customers to extend warranties, or up-sell to the latest product version; proactively engage when potential issues take place in the supply chain process; send communications on behalf of sales and service staff; or build customer loyalty and maintain interest. SAP Customer Data Platform gives you the ability to engage your customers based upon the current state of the account in a highly personalized manner.

 

Conclusion

SAP Customer Data Platform is the only solution in the market that seamlessly brings the power of data from S/4HANA to the front office, where personalized customer engagements become meaningful. In parallel, front-office data is infused in the back-office, where business data decisions can be made, powered by contextual customer insights. Our mission is to Deliver Customer 360 to the Enterprise by Leveraging Operational & Experience Data to Create Intelligent Customer Engagements and Optimized Business Processes.

 

More Information

For further information refer to SAP Customer Data Platform or to SAP Customer Data Platform | SAP Help Portal.

entry.title: How to add fetch multiple lines of record from a CDS view in a BADI and show it in adobe form
entry.content: In SAP Public cloud, I want to fetch multiple lines of record from a CDS VIEW inside a BADI and then show those values in a table inside adobe form. The problem is, the BADI executes only once, and I am able to fetch only one record at a time and show it in the adobe form, what I need is to fetch multiple records at once and display them all in the adobe form template

entry.title: B1 Database Migration SQL >> Hana 9.3 pl14
entry.content:
Dear Expert

We are attempting to migrate SQL database backup from SAP B1 SQL Server to SAP B1 HANA (SAP 9.3 PL 14) Server, but we are encountering an exceptional handling error during the migration process.

Please refer to the attachment for the error reference.

Kindly help to restore the database.

entry.title: Re: RF: Verification profile settings for picking
entry.content:
Dear @Christian_Ries ,

thank you for your reply.

As mentioned initially, I already tried to assign SAP default verification profile SE0005 which contains SKU, quantity, source bin location and destination bin location only to find out if the verification profile is considered in anyway during picking. Which I cannot confirm.

Kind regards,

Josefin

entry.title: Import Sales Orders (Part 9): Creation with Reference
entry.content:
Start From SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.1, by importing data of preceding documents, you can create sales orders with reference to sales quotations or sales contracts. The referenced data can be header data, item data, or both.

The following table lists the fields used for document reference. You can download the import template and add relevant fields to your sales order data sheet.

Data LevelFieldDescriptionUsed For
HeaderReference document (HEADER.REFERENCESDDOCUMENT)Document number of the preceding documentHeader reference
ItemReference document (ITEM.REFERENCESDDOCUMENT)Document number of the preceding documentItem reference
ItemReference item (ITEM.REFERENCESDDOCUMENTITEM)Item number of the preceding documentItem reference

Excel Template:

1. Reference Example

1.1 Only Header Reference

Example: You create a sales order with reference to sales quotation 10060. The sales order header references the sales quotation, whereas the two items are created without reference.

Sales Order (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Header)Item (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Item)Reference Item
10011006001  
10011006002  

Result:

Document NumberItem  Reference Document (Preceding Document)Reference Item(Item of Preceding Document)
0030001001 10060 
003000100110  
003000100120  

1.2 Same Document Referenced on Header and Item Level

Example: You create a sales order with reference to sales quotation 10060. The sales order header references the sales quotation. The first item references item 10 of the sales quotation, whereas the second item is created without reference.

Sales Order (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Header)Item (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Item)Reference Item
10021006001 10
10021006002  

Result:

Document NumberItem  Reference Document (Preceding Document)Reference Item(Item of Preceding Document)
0030001002 10060 
0030001002101006010
003000100220  

1.3 Different Documents Referenced on Header and Item Level

Example:  You create a sales order with reference to sales quotations 10060 and 10070. The sales order header references sales quotation 10060. The first item references item 10 of sales quotation 10070, whereas the second item is created without reference.

Sales Order (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Header)Item (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Item)Reference Item
100310060011007010
10031006002  

Result:

Document NumberItem  Reference Document (Preceding Document)Reference Item                    (Item of Preceding Document)
0030001003 10060 
0030001003101007010
003000100320  

1.4 Only Item Reference

Example: You create a sales order with reference to sales quotation 10060. The sales order header is created without reference. The first item references item 10 of sales quotation 10060, whereas the second item is created without reference.

Sales Order (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Header)Item (Temporary ID)Reference Document (for Item)Reference Item
1004 011006010
1004 02  

Result:

Document NumberItem  Reference Document (Preceding Document)Reference Item                (Preceding Document Item)
0060001004   
0060001004101006010
006000100420  

2. Data Entry Instructions

Due to document reference, you can leave the fields marked in blue empty and must specify the fields marked in yellow.

2.1 Header fields

With header reference, you can leave the sales area (sales organization, distribution channel, division) and sold-to party empty. The system ignores these fields in your file and copies them from the preceding document.

The following table compares how you can enter header data for creation with or without reference.

Reference or NotCredit Memo Request (Temporary ID)Credit Memo Request TypeSales OrganizationDistribution ChannelDivisionSold-to PartyReference Document (for Header)
Without reference1001CR101010005001 
With header reference1002CR    200001

2.2 Item fields

With item reference, you can leave the product-related fields (product, customer material, and GTIN (EAN/UPC)) and the requested quantity empty. The system ignores the product and GTIN in your file and copies these fields from the preceding item. If needed, you can specify the customer material and requested quantity.

If you do not specify a quantity, the system behavior regarding the requested quantity varies with the document type of the preceding document:

  • When a sales quotation or quantity contract is referenced, the system copies the open quantity of the preceding item as the requested quantity during order creation.
  • When a value contract is referenced, the system leaves the requested quantity empty during order creation.

The following table compares how you can enter item data for creation with or without reference.

Reference or NotItem (Temporary ID)ProductCustomer MaterialGTIN (EAN/UPC)Target QuantityReference Document (for Item)Reference Item
Without reference01TG11  1  
With item reference (no quantity specified)02    2000110
With item reference (quantity specified)03   52000120

Useful links:

Blog Series for Import Sales Orders

Help Document

entry.title: Restricting Multiple value using Constraint
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have a situation with Multiple char values to be restricted based on key character maintained in the variant table1.

Char1 - Single Value char

Char2 - Multiple value char

If A is selected in Char1 then Char2 should list 1,2,3 as values in a dropdown to choose. It can be achieved by define constraint net and constraint.

If A is selected in Char1 then Char2 list one of 1,2,3，It cannot be selected at the same time。Is there a way to achieve it？

thanks，

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM Extend with APIs (Part 2): Get started with SAP Cloud ALM API
entry.content:
As the CALM has more than one API better create dedicated service-instances and and service-keys (with API, porpose and privileges (read-only/read-write)) in the name. Also mind that the scope is to specified in the service-instance and that the 

"xsappname": "CALMExtensionAPI",

 xsappname must match the name of the service-instance (SAP_Cloud_ALM_API in your case, not CALMExtensionAPI).

entry.title: Activating SAC on BTP
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

 

where can I find a step-by-step guide to activate the SAC subaccount on BTP?

 

 

thanks

Antonio

entry.title: Re: HANA database Restore in MS SQL Server
entry.content:
There is no way to restore HANA databases on MS SQL SERVER.

You need to export the data from the source tables to files (XML, CSV, ...) or using some tool created to do this kind of task, and then import it on MSSQL. Microsoft have some tools (SSIS, Azure Data Factory) to do this.  If it is a SAP product (like Business Suite), you can use the SWPM heterogenous copy method.

entry.title: Re: HANA instance access within steampunk instance for "S...
entry.content: @DenisPopescu - That could be a possible workaround, but this approach might have some limitations and it's recommended to test it thoroughly before using it in a production environment. Also, keep in mind that additional costs might apply for this.

entry.title: Re: Using SAP Companion Extended Scenario in S/4HANA
entry.content: Thank you @ShaneLipke. If it is technically possible, please could you please share the parameter values how should I set it in Fiori Config. I have tried few ways but i am not able to achieve the scenario of accessing both standard (from SAP Education server S4 workarea) and custom content (Our company custom workarea). Appreciate your support.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Ralph,

find below my reply to your question on a workaround for the IC Supplier Invoice attachment.

After alignment with our technical experts; it's not possible to add the attachement to the ICo supplier invoice by using the BADIs MMIV_IC_ENHANCEMENTS or MMIV_IC_MAPPING.

Maybe you can use a batch report running every night that copies the relevant attachement from the ICo customer invoice to the ICo supplier invoice.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: Fund Center Master data and hierarchy in Group Reporting
entry.content: Hi Florian, Fund Center master data field is not in the list. Do you know ETA for adding this field in Group Reporting scope?

entry.title: How do I auto populate the Payment Terms in FB01 according to vendor number?
entry.content:
Hi, I was trying to populate the Payment Terms according to the vendor number or account number for the first line in FB01. Tried to create a logic in GGB1 but the BSEG inside the custom program was empty. Therefore, I cannot retrieve the vendor number or account number.

Which exit do I need to modify to populate the Payment Terms?

 

Assuming that the Account or Supplier number is 1234567. I am expected that the payment terms will be auto populated with 1010. However, it is empty. How and where do I create logic for that since it BSEG-LIFNR is not present when the custom logic in GGB1 was called.

entry.title: Re: How to control assign purchasing information for posting by plant
entry.content: Hi, Could you please help us, if this issue is fixed?

entry.title: Re: STUSERTRACE: New tracing option (Authorization trace for user)
entry.content: amazing blog, covers everything needed to know.

entry.title: Re: How to control assign purchasing information for posting by plant
entry.content: Internal Debug from ABAP team# When ABAP team performed debug for multiple plant FSD, BADI is not getting triggered. BADI /SCMTMS/BADI_MODIFY_PO_DATA is implemented in PO_CREATE to replace MM data with TM data but error is been triggering in PO_PREPARE. After PO_PREPARE, (if there is no error)PO_CREATE will trigger and inside it BADI will trigger to replace. So we need your help fix the error at PO_PREPARE.

entry.title: Re: How to handle non-cumulative data in Datasphere ?
entry.content:
non-cumulatives  are calculated during query runtime. At least in classical BW. So based on your variable / parameter: date. Either now, or as user variable will the query calculate the the stock. 

During cube / adso compression in BW, will BW create the marker. 

In DSP you can simulate this behavior by calculating the KPI based on the date (either today, or as variable). Or you can do the calculation in SAC, if you use SAC as front end.

However if you just need to push out from DSP, then I would just use an outbound replication flow and move uncompressed (as it comes from S/4) and leave the calculation in runtime during consumption - where ever the data will be consumed later. 

Alternative would be doing the calculation in DSP and pushing out snap-shots. But... Performance? Delta? The premium outbound costs?

entry.title: Re: RF: Verification profile settings for picking
entry.content:
Hi @jschuenemann ,

it should be considered also during picking. Did you make sure to determine the verification profile with the SSCUI Define Warehouse-Specific Verification Determination? Please make sure to maintain both views within that SSCUI (determination & sequence).

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

Looking forward to your reply.

Lesley

entry.title: Re: Not Able to create logos or Texts in output managemen...

entry.title: Re: Offboarding 2.0 Dashboard
entry.content: I have a suggestion only (without any detailed analyes of this issue): You have to create a custom task (ex. end of termination). The user has to complete this task bason on his/here decision and on good time. Until execution of this task the offboarding case is infinished and visible on offboarding dashboard. I applied this solution at my client regarding to the dismissal ticket completion. (The dismissal ticket - it was a paper document earlier - contains more  than 20 custom task items)

entry.title: Re: Remittance Advice - DOC-46: Copy document failed.
entry.content: Hello, I am also facing the same issue but as dencilpaul adviced i dont see any option to keep the payment remittance routing blank we need to select at least one end point pls see attachment

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)

Good Day everyone, 

I am Hira from Pakistan. I fall under women category. I am learning ABAP programming to go forward in my career as technical consultant ABAP/CPI.

I have started my learning journey and would if someone will join me and we work together to share our learning and keep an eye on our goal.

entry.title: Multiple GSTIN for a single supplier
entry.content:
Suppliers might be having GST registrations within different states in India. All the GST numbers should be tracked in Supplier master.

But as per the current system behavior, only one GST Identification Number can be added for India in tax data of supplier master.

How to maintain multiple GST registration number for single supplier?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Migrating from Application Logging Service to Cloud Logging
entry.content:
Shouldn't it be the default and a mandatory requirement that every service provides at least a basic set of observability data for customers?

I personally agree with this and am trying to convince SAP internally. However, there is no obligation for the services to provide data yet. I am therefore happy to receive any support. SAP is customer driven.
If a service gets this feedback by many customers, the likelihood increases that they prioritize providing data. It was also asked several times in a DSAG presentation today, I think there is a customer demand that is not sufficiently recognized by the services.

I also plead for patience, as it takes time to be properly implemented and balanced with other customer requirements.

Best regards
Jürgen

entry.title: Re: Multiple GSTIN for a single supplier
entry.content:
There is no functionality to maintain multiple GSTIN for single supplier.

You need to create the separate supplier master for each GST Identification Number of the single supplier, with different supplying and billing addresses as per need and business requirement.

entry.title: Re: Employment end date notification - old alert
entry.content: Dear Giorgio,

entry.title: Asset depreciation from back-date for additional assets acquired later in current FY and previous FY
entry.content:
Dear Experts 

Greetings 

We need your vital input for the following two business scenario 

(1) 10 number of XYZ asset is acquired in current FY 2024 on 01.04.2024. Depreciation of 10 XYZ assets starts from acquisition date / beginning of current FY 2024 i.e. 01.04.2024. 

On 01.09.2024 business acquired an additional 5 more XYZ assets. At present in SAP S/4 HANA for additional newly acquired 5 XYZ asset depreciation calculation is starting from 01.09.2024. 

The business requirement is that for these later acquired 5 additional XYZ assets also depreciation should start from the beginning of current FY 2024 i.e. from 01.04.2024 not from 01.09.2024. 

(2) 10 number of XYZ asset is acquired in last previous FY 2023 on 01.04.2023. Depreciation of those 10 XYZ assets starts from acquisition date / beginning of previous FY 2023 i.e. 01.04.2023. 

On 01.09.2024 in the current FY 2024, the business acquired an additional 5 more XYZ assets. At present in SAP HANA for additional newly acquired 5 XYZ asset depreciation calculation is starting from 01.09.2024 of current FY 2024. 

The business requirement is that for these later additional acquired 5 XYZ assets also depreciation should start from the beginning of current FY 2024 i.e. from 01.04.2024 not from 01.09.2024. 

 Please share the required input to meet the business requirements in SAP S/4 HANA. 

Thanks and Regards, 

Mayank Vardhan

entry.title: Re: Directly call create object page or RAP action from Tile
entry.content:
@AnmolBhatHD 

You can select "Form Entry Object Page" template when creating a Fiori elements application in Business Application Studio. With this, the app opens in create mode.

https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/533f7e7f59854cb08ce8074814ae83c5

entry.title: Minimum Quantities in REG INFO and MM
entry.content:
Minimum Quantities in REG INFO and MM

We are experiencing issues when processing purchase orders, as the minimum order quantity in the Info Record is purely informational and not considered when placing orders. The data is being pulled from the MRP1 tab. I understand this shouldn't be the case, as we have several suppliers for the same item, and each has a different minimum order quantity.

Please, we need the Info Record to take precedence over the material master data. Could someone assist us with this?

Thank you,
